source.title: Talk It Out, Help Is Here – Friendship Benches

sentences.text: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches.
We have two special guests.
First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda.
Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program?
Sure.
The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist.
One of my patients had become severely depressed.
However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital.
This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before.
In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment.
Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years.
When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people.
I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community.
Oh, so that was the beginning of it.
Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works?
Certainly.
The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services.
It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety.
The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community.
These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage:
On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program?
Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community.
Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works?
Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
choices: the history of psychiatric hospitals and their role in treating depression
the economic challenges faced by elderly women volunteering in African communities
the comparison between traditional and modern approaches to treating anxiety
the origin and operation of a community-based mental health program in Zimbabwe
the importance of government funding for building public park facilities
problems.explanation: 짐바브웨의 정신건강 접근성 문제에서 탄생한 Friendship Bench 프로그램의 기원과 운영 방식(지역사회 할머니들의 무료 상담)을 설명하므로, '짐바브웨의 지역사회 기반 정신건강 프로그램의 탄생과 운영'이 주제이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 요지로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program? Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community. Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works? Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
choices: Mental health treatment should only be provided by professionally trained psychiatrists.
Governments must invest more in building hospitals to solve mental health challenges.
Elderly volunteers are generally less effective than trained professionals at providing therapy.
Community-based approaches can effectively address mental health care gaps where resources are limited.
Depression and anxiety are problems that only affect people in developing countries.
problems.explanation: 정신과 의사 부족과 교통비 문제로 치료를 받지 못하는 현실에서, 지역사회 할머니들을 활용한 무료 상담 프로그램이 효과적 대안이 되었다는 내용이므로, '자원이 부족한 곳에서 지역사회 기반 접근이 정신건강 격차를 해소할 수 있다'가 요지이다.

problems.instruction: Dr. Chibanda가 Friendship Bench 프로그램을 만든 목적으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program? Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community. Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works? Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
choices: to train more professional psychiatrists for hospitals in Zimbabwe
to raise funds for building new hospitals in rural areas of the country
to make mental health services accessible to people who cannot reach hospitals
to conduct research on the causes of depression among elderly women
to replace all hospital-based psychiatric treatment with community volunteers
problems.explanation: 환자가 버스비를 감당할 수 없어 치료를 받지 못하는 경험에서 출발하여, '정신의학을 지역사회로 가져오는 방법을 찾아야 했다'고 했으므로, 병원에 갈 수 없는 사람들에게 정신건강 서비스를 접근 가능하게 하려는 목적이다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program? Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, <u>she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital</u>. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community. Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works? Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
choices: The patient deliberately chose not to seek treatment because she distrusted hospitals.
The hospital refused to treat the patient because her condition was not serious enough.
Public transportation in Zimbabwe is so unreliable that patients prefer to walk to hospitals.
The patient could not find a psychiatrist willing to treat her specific condition.
Financial barriers can prevent people from accessing even basic health care services.
problems.explanation: 버스비를 감당할 수 없어서 병원에 갈 수 없었다는 것은, 경제적 장벽이 기본적인 의료 서비스 접근을 막을 수 있음을 의미한다. ①은 의도적 거부, ③은 병원 거부, ④는 교통 불안정, ⑤는 의사 부재로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program? Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. <u>This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before.</u> In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community. Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works? Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
choices: Dr. Chibanda had always been aware that poverty affected mental health treatment.
Dr. Chibanda was shocked because the patient's depression had worsened unexpectedly.
The problem of unaffordable transportation was a completely new realization for Dr. Chibanda.
The doctor had previously studied the effects of poverty on health care access.
Dr. Chibanda found it difficult to believe that hospitals existed in Zimbabwe.
problems.explanation: '이전에 그런 문제를 고려한 적이 없었기에 충격을 받았다'이므로, 교통비가 치료 접근을 막는다는 사실이 Dr. Chibanda에게 완전히 새로운 깨달음이었다는 의미이다. ①④는 이미 알고 있었다는 반대 의미, ③은 우울증 악화, ⑤는 병원 존재 불신으로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분으로부터 추론할 수 있는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program? Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. <u>When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people.</u> I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community. Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works? Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
choices: Zimbabwe had more psychiatrists per person than most other African countries in 2006.
Eleven psychiatrists were enough to handle the mental health demands of the country.
Zimbabwe's population was too small to justify training additional psychiatrists.
The Friendship Bench program aimed to recruit eleven more psychiatrists by 2010.
The number of psychiatrists in Zimbabwe was vastly insufficient for the population's needs.
problems.explanation: 1,200만 명 이상의 인구에 정신과 의사가 11명뿐이었다는 것은 인구 대비 정신과 의사 수가 극도로 부족했음을 추론할 수 있다. ①은 다른 나라보다 많다는 반대, ③은 충분하다는 반대, ④는 인구가 적다는 반대, ⑤는 의사 추가 채용으로 본문에 없다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 문장이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program? Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. <u>I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community.</u> Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works? Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
choices: Dr. Chibanda wanted to open a new psychiatric hospital inside a residential area.
Community members needed to be trained as licensed psychiatrists to replace hospital doctors.
Dr. Chibanda planned to move all psychiatrists out of hospitals and into community clinics.
Mental health support had to be delivered where people lived rather than only in distant hospitals.
The government had instructed Dr. Chibanda to relocate his practice to a rural village.
problems.explanation: '정신의학을 지역사회로 가져오는 방법을 찾아야 했다'이므로, 멀리 떨어진 병원이 아닌 사람들이 사는 곳에서 정신건강 지원이 이루어져야 한다는 의미이다. ①은 병원 개원, ③은 면허 의사 양성, ④는 모든 의사 이동, ⑤는 정부 지시로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분으로부터 추론할 수 있는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program? Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community. Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works? Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. <u>These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.</u>
choices: The grandmothers receive a high salary for their counseling services at the benches.
The program values empathetic listening and community connection as forms of mental health care.
Only professionally certified therapists are allowed to participate in the program.
The grandmothers prescribe medication to patients who visit the Friendship Benches.
Patients are required to pay a small fee to cover the cost of the therapy sessions.
problems.explanation: 할머니들이 자원봉사로 환자 곁에 앉아 이야기를 들어주고 정서적 지지를 제공한다는 것에서, 공감적 경청과 지역사회 연결을 정신건강 돌봄의 한 형태로 중시함을 추론할 수 있다. ①은 높은 급여, ③은 전문 자격, ④는 약물 처방, ⑤는 유료로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park ________________ in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program? Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community. Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works? Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
problems.explanation: Friendship Benches라는 이름에서 벤치(benches)가 핵심 요소임을 알 수 있다. fences(울타리), bridges(다리), fountains(분수), shelters(대피소)는 프로그램명과 일치하지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. The idea came from an experience I had as a ________________. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. problems.explanation: 환자(patient)가 심한 우울증에 걸렸다는 맥락에서 정신과 의사(psychiatrist)가 적절하다. journalist(기자), politician(정치인), architect(건축가), economist(경제학자)는 환자 치료와 관련 없다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?

problems.explanation: come up with = '고안하다, 생각해내다'. 프로그램을 창안한 배경을 묻는 질문이므로 적절. give up on(포기하다), look down on(무시하다), get away from(도망치다), put up with(참다)는 문맥과 맞지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?

problems.blankTarget: she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital
choices: the hospital was closed for renovations during that period
she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital
her family members refused to let her see a doctor
there were no psychiatric medications available in the country
she had already recovered from her depression on her own
problems.explanation: 원문에서 환자가 병원까지 가는 버스비를 감당할 수 없었기 때문에 도움을 받지 못했다고 한다. ①은 병원 폐쇄, ③은 가족 거부, ④는 약물 없음, ⑤는 스스로 회복으로 본문과 다르다.

The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
problems.explanation: 버스비를 감당할 수 없다는 앞 문장에 이어, '교통비를 지불하고 의료 치료를 받는 것'이 어렵다는 일반적 진술이다. ①은 근처 의사 찾기, ②는 증상 전달, ④는 의료 기록 이해, ⑤는 온라인 예약으로 문맥과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program? Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community. Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works? Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues _____________________________. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
choices: related to financial planning and debt management
concerning physical injuries from workplace accidents
involving legal disputes with neighbors or employers
stemming from poor academic performance at school
problems.explanation: 정신건강 서비스 프로그램이므로 우울증이나 불안 같은 정신건강 문제를 다루는 것이 적절. ①은 재정, ③은 신체 부상, ④는 법적 분쟁, ⑤는 학업 성적으로 정신건강 서비스와 직접 관련 없다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program? Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community. Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works? Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," ________________ to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
problems.explanation: 무료 세션을 제공하는 존경받는 여성들이므로 자원봉사(volunteer)가 적절. refuse(거부), hesitate(주저), struggle(고군분투), pretend(가장하다)는 문맥과 맞지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before.
problems.passage: The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. ( ① ) In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. ( ② ) Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. ( ③ ) When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. ( ④ )
problems.explanation: 환자가 버스비를 감당할 수 없어 도움을 받지 못했다는 문장 직후, 이에 충격을 받았다는 반응이 ①에 오는 것이 자연스럽다. 'such a problem'이 앞의 버스비 문제를 가리킨다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years.
problems.passage: This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. ( ① ) When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. ( ② ) I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community. ( ③ ) The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. ( ④ )
problems.explanation: 교통비 문제에 더해(Furthermore) 정신과 의사 부족 문제를 추가하는 것이 ①에서 자연스럽다. 이어서 2006년의 구체적 통계가 이 부족 상황을 뒷받침한다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community.
problems.passage: The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. ( ① ) It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. ( ② ) These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support. ( ③ )
problems.explanation: 상담 세션에 대한 설명 후, 세션이 무료이며 지역사회 여성들이 제공한다는 정보가 ②에 와야, 'These women'이 바로 이 여성들을 가리키며 자연스럽게 이어진다.

problems.instruction: 주어진 글 다음에 이어질 글의 순서로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.givenStart: The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist.

chunks.A: This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before.
chunks.B: One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital.
chunks.C: In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment.

problems.explanation: (B) 환자의 구체적 사례(우울증, 버스비 문제) → (A) 'This'가 (B)의 상황을 받아 충격 반응 → (C) 'In fact'로 이어지며 일반적 상황으로 확대. 'This'와 'such a problem'이 (B)를 가리키므로 (B)-(A) 순서 필수.

problems.instruction: 주어진 글 다음에 이어질 글의 순서로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.givenStart: Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works? Certainly.

chunks.A: It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety.
chunks.B: These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
chunks.C: The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community.

problems.explanation: 프로그램이 무엇인지 설명(A: 접근 가능한 정신건강 서비스 → 상담 세션 제공)이 먼저 오고, (C: 무료이며 지역사회 여성 제공) → (B: 'These women' 구체 설명). 'These women'이 (C)의 elderly women을 받으므로 (C)-(B) 순서 필수.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하는 것은?
problems.passage:
On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program?
Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community.
choices: The Friendship Bench program was started in Zimbabwe in 2016.
Dr. Chibanda created the program because a patient could not afford to travel to the hospital.
Zimbabwe had over a hundred psychiatrists when the program began.
The patient chose not to visit the hospital because she disliked the treatment there.
Dr. Chibanda had always been aware of the transportation problem before starting the program.
problems.explanation: ⑦⑧번 문장에서 환자가 버스비를 감당할 수 없어 도움을 받지 못한 경험이 프로그램의 계기가 되었다. ②는 2006년이 맞고 2016년이 아님, ③은 11명이지 100명 이상이 아님, ④는 치료 불만이 아닌 경제적 이유, ⑤는 이전에 고려한 적 없었다고 했으므로 틀림.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하는 것은?
problems.passage: The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
choices: Respected elderly women from the community provide the free talk therapy sessions.
The therapy sessions at the Friendship Benches require a small fee from patients.
The program provides medication rather than talk therapy for patients with depression.
The "grandmothers" are professionally trained psychiatrists who work at the benches.
Only young volunteers under the age of thirty are eligible to work in the program.
problems.explanation: ⑲⑳번 문장에서 세션은 무료이며 지역사회의 존경받는 나이 든 여성들이 제공한다고 했다. ①은 유료가 아닌 무료, ②는 약물이 아닌 상담, ③은 전문 의사가 아닌 자원봉사자, ⑤는 젊은 봉사자가 아닌 나이 든 여성이므로 틀림.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people.
choices: The Friendship Benches are located in Zimbabwe.
Dr. Chibanda's patient was unable to afford the bus fare to the hospital.
Dr. Chibanda had frequently dealt with the problem of patients' transportation costs before.
Many people in Zimbabwe find it hard to pay for transportation and medical care.
There were only eleven psychiatrists for Zimbabwe's twelve-million-plus population in 2006.
problems.explanation: ⑨번 문장에서 'I had never considered such a problem before(이전에 그런 문제를 고려한 적이 없었다)'고 했으므로, '자주 다루어 왔다'는 ③이 본문과 일치하지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage: The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
choices: The grandmothers are paid employees hired by the government to staff the benches.
The Friendship Bench program was designed to make mental health services accessible.
Talk therapy sessions are offered to people experiencing depression or anxiety.
The sessions are provided at no cost to the people who use them.
The grandmothers listen to patients' stories and offer emotional support.
problems.explanation: ⑳번 문장에서 할머니들은 '자원봉사(volunteer)'한다고 했으므로, '정부가 고용한 유급 직원'이라는 ③이 본문과 일치하지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage:
On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program?
Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community.
Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works?
Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
choices: Dr. Dixon Chibanda is the founder of the Friendship Bench program.
Zimbabwe had a sufficient number of psychiatrists when the program started in 2006.
The program was inspired by a patient who could not afford hospital transportation.
The Friendship Bench program offers free talk therapy for depression and anxiety.
Community elderly women called "grandmothers" provide emotional support at the benches.
problems.explanation: ⑪⑫번 문장에서 정신과 의사가 부족했으며 1,200만 명에 11명뿐이었다고 했으므로, '충분한 수의 정신과 의사가 있었다'는 ③이 본문과 일치하지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A)와 (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community.
problems.summaryTemplate: Dr. Chibanda was inspired to create the Friendship Bench program after realizing that ____(A)____ barriers and a ____(B)____ of psychiatrists prevented many Zimbabweans from getting mental health care.
problems.explanation: 교통비(financial barriers)와 정신과 의사 부족(shortage of psychiatrists)이 핵심 동기이다. ①의 cultural/surplus, ③의 political/network, ④의 linguistic/tradition, ⑤의 surplus는 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A)와 (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
problems.summaryTemplate: The Friendship Bench program provides ____(A)____ therapy by trained community ____(B)____ to make mental health support accessible to all.
problems.explanation: 상담(talk) 치료를 지역사회 자원봉사자(volunteers)인 할머니들이 제공한다는 내용과 일치. ①의 physical/doctors, ③의 drug/nurses, ④의 group/students, ⑤의 officials는 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 This가 가리키는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program? Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. <u>This</u> shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community. Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works? Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
choices: the fact that the patient's depression had suddenly worsened overnight
the situation in which a patient could not access help due to transportation costs
the discovery that hospitals in Zimbabwe lacked proper medical equipment
the realization that psychiatrists were being paid too little for their services
the news that the government had cut funding for all mental health programs
problems.explanation: This는 바로 앞 문장의 '환자가 교통비 때문에 도움을 받을 수 없었던 상황'을 가리킨다. ①은 우울증 악화, ②는 장비 부족, ④는 급여 부족, ⑤는 정부 예산 삭감으로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 it이 가리키는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up w<u>it</u>h the program? Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community. Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works? Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. These women, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
choices: the host's television career in broadcasting
the shortage of psychiatrists in Zimbabwe
the construction of new hospitals in rural areas
problems.explanation: 진행자가 'that was the beginning of it'이라고 한 것은 Dr. Chibanda가 설명한 경험이 Friendship Bench 프로그램의 시작이었다는 의미이다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 These women이 가리키는 대상으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called Friendship Benches. We have two special guests. First, please welcome the founder of the Friendship Bench program, Dr. Dixon Chibanda. Can you tell us what inspired you to come up with the program? Sure. The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. One of my patients had become severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was unable to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, as I had never considered such a problem before. In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation and receive medical treatment. Furthermore, there has been a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years. When the Friendship Bench program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists serving Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people. I needed to find a way to bring psychiatry into the community. Oh, so that was the beginning of it. Could you explain to us how the Friendship Bench program works? Certainly. The program was created as a way to provide accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people dealing with issues such as depression or anxiety. The sessions are free and are provided by respected elderly women from the community. <u>These women</u>, called "grandmothers," volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
choices: professional psychiatrists who graduated from medical schools in Zimbabwe
government officials responsible for public health policy in the country
young female students training to become mental health counselors
international aid workers sent by foreign organizations to assist Zimbabwe
respected elderly women from the community who provide the free sessions
problems.explanation: These women은 바로 앞 문장의 'respected elderly women from the community(지역사회의 존경받는 나이 든 여성들)'를 가리킨다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going ①<u>to learn</u> about special park benches in Zimbabwe ②<u>called</u> Friendship Benches. Can you tell us what ③<u>inspiring</u> you to come up with the program? The idea came from an experience I had as a psychiatrist. It is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe ④<u>to pay</u> for transportation. The program ⑤<u>was created</u> as a way to provide accessible mental health services.
problems.explanation: inspire + O + to V에서 '무엇이 영감을 주었는지'를 묻는 간접의문문이므로 과거형 inspired가 적절하다. inspiring → inspired. ① be going to V ✓ ② 과거분사 후치수식(called) ✓ ④ it is difficult + to V ✓ ⑤ 수동태 was created ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: One of my patients ①<u>had become</u> severely depressed. However, she couldn't get help because she was ②<u>unable</u> to afford the bus ride to the hospital. This shocked me, ③<u>as</u> I had never considered such a problem before. Furthermore, there ④<u>has been</u> a lack of psychiatrists in the country. When the program started in 2006, there were only eleven psychiatrists ⑤<u>serving</u> Zimbabwe's population of over twelve million people.
problems.explanation: 문맥상 과거 시점 이전부터의 상태를 나타내므로 과거완료 had been이 적절하다. has been → had been. ① 과거완료(had become) ✓ ② be unable to V ✓ ③ 이유의 접속사 as ✓ ⑤ 현재분사 수식(serving) ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: Could you explain to us ①<u>how</u> the Friendship Bench program works? The program was created as a way ②<u>to provide</u> accessible mental health services. It offers talk therapy sessions to people ③<u>dealing</u> with issues such as depression. The sessions are free and ④<u>are provided</u> by respected elderly women. These women volunteer to sit with patients, ⑤<u>listening</u> to their stories, and provide them with emotional support.
problems.explanation: sit, listen, provide가 병렬구조(volunteer to sit, listen, and provide)이므로 동사원형 listen이 적절하다. listening → listen. ① 간접의문문 how ✓ ② a way to V(형용사적 용법) ✓ ③ 현재분사 수식(dealing with) ✓ ④ 수동태 are provided ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches ①<u>calling</u> Friendship Benches. The idea came from an experience ②<u>I had</u> as a psychiatrist. She was unable ③<u>to afford</u> the bus ride to the hospital. It is difficult for many people ④<u>to pay</u> for transportation. I needed to find a way ⑤<u>to bring</u> psychiatry into the community.
problems.explanation: 벤치가 '불리는' 것이므로 수동의 의미인 과거분사 called가 적절하다. calling → called. ② 관계대명사 생략(experience that I had) ✓ ③ be unable to V ✓ ④ it is difficult + to V ✓ ⑤ a way to V ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 to부정사의 용법이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: What inspired you <u>to come up</u> with the program?
She was unable <u>to afford</u> the bus ride to the hospital.
It is difficult for many people <u>to pay</u> for transportation.
I needed to find a way <u>to bring</u> psychiatry into the community.
The program was created as a way <u>to provide</u> accessible mental health services.
problems.explanation: ④는 명사적 용법(가주어 it의 진주어). ①은 명사적 용법(목적격보어), ②는 형용사적 용법(unable 수식)이지만, ③⑤는 형용사적 용법(a way를 수식). 그러나 ①②와 ④를 비교하면: ①은 inspire + O + to V(결과/목적 보어), ②는 unable to V(형용사 보충), ③⑤는 a way to V(형용사적). ④만 가주어 구문의 진주어(명사적 용법)로 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 as의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: The idea came from an experience I had <u>as</u> a psychiatrist.
This shocked me, <u>as</u> I had never considered such a problem before.
The program was created <u>as</u> a way to provide mental health services.
He works <u>as</u> a teacher at a local school.
She was recognized <u>as</u> the best researcher in the field.
problems.explanation: ②의 as는 이유의 접속사('~이므로'). 나머지 ①③④⑤는 모두 전치사 as('~로서, ~으로')이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 that의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: Oh, so <u>that</u> was the beginning of it.
I believe <u>that</u> community support is essential for mental health.
She told us <u>that</u> the sessions are completely free.
The doctor explained <u>that</u> many people cannot afford treatment.
We realized <u>that</u> a new approach was needed.
problems.explanation: ①의 that은 지시대명사('그것'), 나머지 ②③④⑤는 모두 접속사 that(명사절을 이끄는)이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 분사의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: people <u>dealing</u> with issues such as depression
The news was very <u>shocking</u> to the doctor.
problems.explanation: ⑤의 shocking은 서술적 용법(보어로서 감정 유발 형용사). 나머지 ①②③④는 모두 후치수식(명사를 뒤에서 수식하는 분사)이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe calling Friendship Benches.
problems.explanation: 벤치가 '불리는' 것(수동)이므로 과거분사 called가 적절하다. calling은 능동의 현재분사이므로 부적절.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: Can you tell us what inspired you coming up with the program?
problems.explanation: inspire + 목적어 + to V. inspire는 to부정사를 목적격 보어로 취한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: The idea came from an experience which I had it as a psychiatrist.
problems.explanation: 관계대명사 which가 had의 목적어 역할을 하므로 대명사 it은 중복이다. 관계대명사절 안에서 선행사를 다시 대명사로 받지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: She couldn't get help because she was unable affording the bus ride to the hospital.
problems.explanation: be unable to V. unable 뒤에는 to부정사가 온다. 동명사(affording)는 쓸 수 없다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: In fact, it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe paying for transportation and receive medical treatment.
problems.explanation: it is difficult for ~ to V(가주어-진주어 구문). 진주어 자리에 to부정사가 와야 한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: Could you explain to us that the Friendship Bench program works?
problems.explanation: explain how S + V(간접의문문). '어떻게 작동하는지'를 묻고 있으므로 의문부사 how가 적절하다. that은 '~라는 것'이라는 사실을 전달할 뿐 방법을 묻지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: These women volunteer to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and providing them with emotional support.
problems.explanation: sit, listen, provide가 to에 연결된 병렬구조(volunteer to sit, listen, and provide)이므로 모두 동사원형이어야 한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: The sessions are free and provided from respected elderly women from the community.
problems.explanation: 수동태에서 행위자를 나타낼 때 전치사 by를 쓴다. be provided by ~.

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: On today's show, _________________________________ Friendship Benches.
problems.answer: we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe called
problems.explanation: be going to V: we are going to learn about / 장소: in Zimbabwe / 과거분사 후치수식: called Friendship Benches

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.answer: what inspired you to come up with
problems.explanation: 간접의문문: what inspired you / inspire + O + to V: to come up with. '무엇이 당신에게 프로그램을 고안하도록 영감을 주었는지.'

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: In fact, _________________________________ and receive medical treatment.
problems.answer: it is difficult for many people in Zimbabwe to pay for transportation
problems.explanation: 가주어 it + is difficult + 의미상 주어 for many people in Zimbabwe + 진주어 to pay for transportation

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: I needed _________________________________ into the community.
problems.answer: to find a way to bring psychiatry
problems.explanation: need + to V: to find / a way to V: a way to bring / bring A into B: psychiatry into the community

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.answer: explain to us how the Friendship Bench program
problems.explanation: explain to + IO(us) + 간접의문문(how + S + V): how the Friendship Bench program works

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: These women volunteer _________________________________ with emotional support.
problems.answer: to sit with patients at the benches, listen to their stories, and provide them
problems.explanation: volunteer to V: to sit / 병렬구조: sit, listen, and provide / 장소: at the benches

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: On today's show, we are going to learn about special park benches in Zimbabwe _______ (call) Friendship Benches.
problems.explanation: 벤치가 '불리는' 것(수동)이므로 과거분사 called가 후치수식으로 적절하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: Can you tell us what inspired you _______ (come) up with the program?
problems.explanation: inspire + 목적어 + to V. inspire는 to부정사를 목적격 보어로 취한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: One of my patients _______ (become) severely depressed before I met her.
problems.explanation: 과거 시점(만남) 이전에 이미 우울증이 심해져 있었으므로 과거완료 had become이 적절하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: She was unable _______ (afford) the bus ride to the hospital.
problems.explanation: be unable to V. unable 뒤에는 to부정사가 온다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: This shocked me, as I _______ (never / consider) such a problem before.
problems.explanation: 과거 시점(shocked) 이전의 경험 부재를 나타내므로 과거완료 had never considered가 적절하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: Furthermore, there _______ (be) a lack of psychiatrists in the country for many years.
problems.explanation: for many years와 함께 현재까지 지속되는 상태를 나타내므로 현재완료 has been이 적절하다. there + has been + 단수명사(a lack).

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: The program _______ (create) as a way to provide accessible mental health services.
problems.explanation: 프로그램이 '만들어진' 것(수동)이므로 수동태 was created가 적절하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: It offers talk therapy sessions to people _______ (deal) with issues such as depression or anxiety.
problems.explanation: people을 수식하는 현재분사 dealing이 적절하다. 사람들이 '다루고 있는(능동·진행)' 문제이므로 현재분사 후치수식.
